import pytest
from source import find_first_in_sorted

def test_find_first_in_sorted_target_not_present():
    """
    The buggy implementation incorrectly sets the upper bound to len(arr) and
    never reduces it when a target is not found, leading to an IndexError
    or infinite loop.  When the target is greater than any element in the
    array, the function should return -1.  This test verifies that behavior.
    """
    arr = [1, 2, 3]
    target = 4  # greater than any element in arr
    assert find_first_in_sorted(arr, target) == -1, (
        "Expected -1 for a target not present in the array, but got "
        f"{find_first_in_sorted(arr, target)}"
    )
